scenario.description: Motivated buyer, neutral market, inspection issues discovered, seller has made two price reductions

listing.sellerSituation: Seller has already purchased new home — carrying two mortgages. Motivated to close.

negotiationTargetItems.negotiationApproach: Request credit or price reduction — roof is at end of life and confirmed by inspector
Request credit — safety issue, some insurers will not insure with this panel type
Negotiate credit — pending specialist evaluation, use midpoint estimate
Use as additional negotiating chip — less urgent than roof/electric/basement but adds to pattern

recommendedStrategy.rationale: 64 days on market with two price cuts signals seller fatigue. Seller is carrying two mortgages — motivated. Inspection revealed ~$19K–$49K in needed repairs, with a reasonable $23.5K negotiation target on clear-cut items. Comps support $270K–$282K for this condition. Offering $268K leaves room to negotiate up while accounting for repair costs. Even if accepted at ask ($289K), the repair burden makes the true cost $308K+.

alternativeApproach.rationale: If seller resists credits, offer $271K firm (price reduction achieves similar result). Simpler for financing.

earnestMoney.notes: 1% of offer price is standard in this market. Do not go above $5K given number of unknowns.

closeDate.notes: Give seller a clean 45-day close — they need time, offering certainty on timeline is a non-cash concession that can help your offer.

contingencyAdvice.notes: Do not waive inspection contingency given the known issues. This is not a multiple-offer situation — you have no competitive pressure to waive anything.

escalationClause.notes: No known competing offers and 64 DOM — no escalation clause needed.
